channel.title: Tech
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Tech, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: India’s Pronto formalizes house help as its valuation jumps 8x in under a year
item.description: Pronto handles 18,000 daily bookings and plans rapid expansion as it races rivals to digitize India's largely offline home services market.

item.title: ChatGPT uninstalls surged by 295% after DoD deal
item.description: Many consumers ditched ChatGPT's app after news of its DoD deal went live, while Claude's downloads grew.

item.title: Cursor has reportedly surpassed $2B in annualized revenue
item.description: The four-year-old startup saw its revenue run rate double over the past three months, according to one Bloomberg source.

item.title: Stripe wants to turn your AI costs into a profit center
item.description: Stripe released a preview intended to allow AI companies to easily track, pass through, and make a profit on underlying AI model fees.

item.title: When the Internet Goes Dark, the Truth Goes With It
item.description: Palestinian journalist Plestia Alaqad on bearing witness, the fragile power of social media, and why documenting lived reality matters more than ever.

item.title: Best AirPods (2026): Which Apple Headphones Should You Buy?
item.description: From the AirPods Pro to the AirPods Max, these are the best headphones for your iPhone and beyond.

item.title: A Former Top Trump Official Is Going After Prediction Markets
item.description: As the battle over prediction markets rages on, Mick Mulvaney is leading a new coalition that calls these platforms illegal gambling.

item.title: The Data Centers Have Arrived at the Edge of the Arctic Circle
item.description: As AI labs gorge themselves on compute, data center operators have headed north in search of cheap and plentiful energy.

item.title: Geopolitical drama reportedly stalls IPO of SoftBank-backed PayPay
item.description: The Japan-based company reportedly aimed for a public valuation of at least ¥1.5 trillion ($10 billion).

item.title: Lenovo’s Latest Wacky Concepts Include a Laptop With a Built-in Portable Monitor
item.description: At MWC 2026, the company also showed off a dual-screen Yoga Book with 3D capabilities, and the Legion Go Fold—a gaming handheld with a folding screen ...
